element_introduction_order.mastery_context: foundational control of descent and speed management
penalty system: wrong side of flag causes snowplow/slowdown
airborne state triggers, enables freestyle trick opportunities
bonus points available; failed tricks cause tumble/time loss
unpredictable collision avoidance, increases spatial complexity
earned through qualifying points; higher difficulty, time pressure intensifies
increased obstacle density and speed expectations

typical_relief_triggers: reaching finish line within time limit
accumulating points for solo run bonus time
typical_tension_triggers: approaching flags (need to navigate correct side)
solo run mode (no qualifying crutch, pure skill test)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: wave-based: alternating between relaxed navigation zones and concentrated obstacle sequences; tension escalates across three difficulty-tiered mountains; solo runs break rhythm with high-stakes pressure segments

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: moguls and trick opportunities are optional skill expressions; player can choose safe descent vs. risky airborne maneuvers for bonus points

difficulty_curve.early_game: Snowy Hill: foundational obstacle types (static trees, basic flags, gentle moguls); establishes core mechanics without overwhelming density; linear introduction of mechanics across 8 runs
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Steep Peak: intermediate challenge; increased obstacle density and collision frequency; moguls and dynamic obstacles appear more often; 8 runs with compounding complexity
difficulty_curve.late_game: Mount Nasty: expert difficulty; maximum obstacle concentration and speed demands; solo run conversions become more frequent; 8 runs representing mastery test
notable_spikes: first solo run after earning qualifying points (shift from guided to self-reliant pacing)
transition from Snowy Hill to Steep Peak (sudden density increase)
transition from Steep Peak to Mount Nasty (collision hazard maximization)

notable_level_patterns.description: Qualifying runs feature other skiers and relaxed time limits; successful point accumulation unlocks solo runs where player races alone with converted points as bonus time. Creates rhythm shift and difficulty spike within same mountain.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Any transition between runs within a mountain

notable_level_patterns.description: Game offers three selectable mountains (Snowy Hill, Steep Peak, Mount Nasty) allowing player choice of difficulty progression. Each mountain contains 8 runs with escalating complexity within its tier.

Early runs use sparse, isolated obstacles; mid-runs cluster obstacles together; late-runs maximize collision hazards and require rapid decision-making. Static obstacles give way to dynamic obstacle mixing.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Snowy Hill Run 1 vs. Mount Nasty Run 8

notable_level_patterns.description: Moguls present optional airborne state where players can perform tricks for bonus points but risk tumbles if failed. Encourages replay and mastery-driven replayability within single runs.
